title: Market Brief — Tuesday, April 7, 2026
summary: Compute is severely scarce on H200 at just 7% availability (121 of 405 GPUs) with spot prices surging 7.8% day-over-day to $2.32/hr, the sharpest move across all classes. H100 sits at 17% availability (80 of 293), also in shortage territory, while B200 is the only relief valve at 25% (72 of 315) despite softening utilization down 9.1pp this month. The H200 squeeze is the story: 93.1% utilization and climbing fast.
paragraphs: Compute is severely scarce on H200 at just 7% availability (121 of 405 GPUs) with spot prices surging 7.8% day-over-day to $2.32/hr, the sharpest move across all classes. H100 sits at 17% availability (80 of 293), also in shortage territory, while B200 is the only relief valve at 25% (72 of 315) despite softening utilization down 9.1pp this month. The H200 squeeze is the story: 93.1% utilization and climbing fast.

On TCO-adjusted H100 cost of $6.83/M tokens, medium-tier API sellers are underwater by $3.49/M tokens, and even high-tier barely breaks even at 9% margin. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.38/M output is aggressively burning cash against any self-hosted baseline. OpenAI's o3 Pro at $80/M output prints money at ultra-premium. Grades: OpenAI Strong, Anthropic Neutral, Google Neutral, DeepSeek Burning Cash.

B200 spot at $3.94/hr is overvalued against its OCPI fair value of $4.13/hr given 25% availability and falling utilization, meaning supply is loosening before the market has repriced. H200 capacity at 7% availability is deeply undervalued relative to demand trajectory, making locked-in H200 reservations the clearest long trade in GPU compute today.
bullets: 7% of H200 GPUs are available, the tightest supply across all three compute classes.
17% H100 availability signals continued shortage with utilization climbing 8.4pp in 30 days.
$2.32/hr H200 spot jumped 7.8% in a single day on extreme scarcity.
96% annualized volatility on B200 OCPI makes it the riskiest near-term GPU position.
$0.38/M output from DeepSeek V3.2 sits roughly 80% below TCO-adjusted H100 production cost.
21.41 on the LLMflation Index means frontier inference costs have fallen 79% since GPT-4 launch.
